Final Theme: Chose actions with greater instrumental utility
Definition: Made decisions with the intention of reducing the time or energy needed to complete the task, and/or increasing the amount of points that could be earned.
Example: “I chose to complete the task alone when I felt it would be quicker than doing it together.” (Participant 108)
“Doing the task alone is more efficient and requires less effort…” (Participant 207)
“[I chose alone] because it would be easier [to] do it on my own. Doing it together entails greater cognitive load.” (Participant 205)
“[I chose alone] when I thought it would be hard to coordinate to hit the dots at the same time” (Participant 105)

Initial Theme: Completed the task in the “easiest” way
Example: “I chose to do the task alone to eliminate the boxes faster as it was easier for me to do it alone.” (Participant 403)

Initial Theme: Considered the spatial arrangement of the boxes
Example: “I would choose to complete the task alone when the boxes were seen as farther apart. When the boxes were closer together, I believed that it would be easier to complete the task together.” (Participant 208)
“[I chose together’ when there wasn't many boxes on the screen.” (Participant 101)
“I chose to do the task alone more often because it would give us higher points.” (Participant 311)

Final Theme: Chose actions with greater social value
Definition: Made decisions that were motivated by enhancing their own and/or their partner's  experience.
Example: “I chose to do it together because I didn't want to seem rude for never choosing to do it together…” (Participant 210)
“I chose to do the task together because it was fun when we did it together…” (Participant 201)
“I usually didn't want the helper to feel left out and wanted to get them as many opportunities as possible” (Participant 407)
“When there were a lot of alone trials in a row sometimes I would switch it up to make it a together trial.” (Participants 401)

Definition: Made decisions knowing they could perform the task consistently well in that task mode, sometimes influenced by performance in previous trials.
Example: “I would choose to complete the task alone because at times I trusted my own judgment, methods, and coordination alone more than my partner's ability to synchronize with me.” (Participant 308)
“I chose to do the tasks alone when I felt that the helper made a series of errors during previous runs.” (Participant 111)

Definition: Conveyed their own or inferred their partner's intentionality through sensorimotor communication strategies or observed improved partner dynamics.
Example: “To practice, after a "together" task took a bit more time or led to some confusion between us.” (Participant 104)

“[I chose together] when I was able to understand what boxes he would move his cursor [to] first so I was able to get the hang of it and we were able to finish boxes together at a faster rate.” (Participant 410)

Definition: Discussed their own individual directional patterns they typically moved in and/or the order they planned to clear boxes in.
Example: “Most tasks, I chose to do it from right to left (right-handed), or in proximity- closest to farthest.” (Participant 213)

Definition: Developed a preference to complete the task alone or together throughout the experiment.
Example: “If there were a greater amount of boxes, I would choose to complete the task alone. Towards the end of the experiment, I would always choose to complete the task alone if the option was given.“ (Participant 207)
